No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course addresses 'human cognitive activities: multitasking, stress and workload,' directly linking to promoting mental health and well-being (Target 3.4) by designing user-friendly systems.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops relevant technical skills for employment (Target 4.4) in UX and human-automation design.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to clean water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops skills in 'information technology' and 'intelligent/mobile systems' design, contributing to 'technological upgrading and innovation' (Target 8.2) and 'decent job creation' (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core focus on 'human-automation design' and 'intelligent and mobile systems' directly enhances 'scientific research' and 'technological capabilities' (Target 9.5) and fosters 'business innovation'.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course addresses 'Individual differences' and 'International usability: cultural differences,' promoting inclusive design and accessibility for all users (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The study of 'navigation applications' has a weak but relevant link to developing accessible and sustainable transport systems (Target 11.2) in urban environments.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to responsible consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The core competence 'Social responsibility and ethics' provides a minimal link to designing systems that support accountable and non-discriminatory institutions (Targets 16.6, 16.b).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to global partnerships for sustainable development.
